meta.draftNote: Assembled 2026-05-24 from three external sources (Nov 2024 Stratpoint docx, May 2024 PDF, portfolio SPA chunks) plus two user-authored in-repo briefs: docs/cv-sources/stratpoint-current-role-2026-05-24.md (current Stratpoint workstreams) and docs/cv-sources/freelance-projects-2026-05-24.md (Locums Nurse / Hockey Time / Stashville).

basics.name: Dave Charm Bulaquena
basics.label: Full Stack Developer & Solutions Designer
basics.summary: Solutions Designer and Full Stack Developer who bridges technical implementation, architecture, and user-centered design. Currently at Stratpoint driving pre-sales solutioning (NextERP, workforce-management, MDM) and internal initiatives (AI-assisted delivery process mapping, AI enablement training). Hands-on engineer + architect on a Shopify + React/Remix e-commerce venture (Zoomy For Pets / Cedar Tree Brands Inc.), building custom analytics plugins and shipping with Claude Code-driven AI-assisted development. Freelance side-project depth across healthcare (React Native locums-nurse app), sports SaaS (Next.js + Stripe + Inngest tournament platform), and developer tooling (Go + Pixi.js gamified tracker with custom SDK and daemon). Prior production depth in cross-platform mobile (React Native, Flutter, native Android/Java) on fintech and retail apps. Comfortable shipping end-to-end on solo projects — from Arduino capstone hardware to LLM-tooling pipelines.

work.name: Stratpoint Global Outsourcing Inc.
work.summary: Architecture and Governance Team — driving pre-sales solutioning and internal initiatives spanning MDM vendor selection, AI-assisted delivery process mapping, NextERP and workforce-management pre-sales, an active Shopify + Remix e-commerce venture (Zoomy For Pets / Cedar Tree Brands Inc.), the AI enablement training program, and re-architecting the F1 internal resource-management system.
highlights: MDM Solution Initiative — drove the company-wide Mobile Device Management initiative end-to-end: owned the research, analysis, and qualification of candidate MDM vendors, then presented the recommended solution and trade-offs to the company.

AI Delivery-Process Initiative — mapped Stratpoint's end-to-end delivery process from pre-sales through project closing, identified where AI can compress or replace work in each stage, and recommended specific tools and the resulting process changes for each step.
NextERP pre-sales — collaborated on solutioning and co-authored the solutions document for a NextERP engagement; mapped client business requirements to a solution targeting their stated pain points.

Workforce Management System (WFMS) pre-sales — collaborated on solutioning and co-authored the solutions document; mapped business requirements to an architecture that addresses the client's operational pain points.

Zoomy For Pets / Cedar Tree Brands Inc. venture — wearing engineer + architect hat on the e-commerce application; built on Shopify with React + Remix, including custom Shopify app plugins for analytics. AI-assisted delivery using Claude Code throughout development.

AI Enablement Training (first-cohort trainer) — one of the first internal trainers responsible for upskilling employees on AI usage. Authored and curated a prompt-compilation, skills, and agent library targeted at repetitive employee tasks so individual contributors can adopt AI without bespoke prompt engineering.
F1 — internal resource-management system for resource requests: tasked with revamping the project's solution and architecture (partial engagement).
Craft technical proposals, system blueprints, and integration strategies for pre-sales — directly influencing deal closures and onboarding of new enterprise clients.
Bridge technical feasibility with business value, ensuring alignment between stakeholder goals and system capabilities.

work.name: Clearminds Algorithmic Incorporated (Universal Storefront Service Corporation)
work.summary: Cross-platform mobile developer shipping features across financial-services and retail products — USSC Super Service App, USSC Business Solution (UBS Pay1st), and Go Salads POS.
highlights: USSC Super Service App (consumer remittance/bills-payment/prepaid app, nationwide): engineered cross-platform enhancements in React Native, optimizing performance and iOS/Android compatibility.
USSC Super Service App: full refactor of the bank remittance transfer feature, achieving faster transactions and smoother user interactions.
USSC Super Service App: crafted a new transaction interface that simplified the flow and made financial transactions more intuitive for end users.
USSC Super Service App: diagnosed and eliminated a crash-class bug affecting a subset of users, materially improving app stability.
USSC Super Service App: modernized the codebase against the latest React Native practices, raising maintainability and future-proofing the product.
USSC Business Solution (UBS Pay1st, B2B international payments app, 800+ U-Store network): upgraded the project to the latest Expo SDK, unlocking new platform features and improved performance.
USSC Business Solution: designed and implemented the transaction flow and payment-processing UI, enhancing UX and shortening transaction steps.
USSC Business Solution: integrated core-service APIs enabling real-time data exchange between app and backend.

Go Salads POS (in-store point-of-sale + inventory + sales-insights app): provided critical production support; resolved a connectivity bug affecting lower-version Android devices, broadening device-stability coverage.

work.name: Stratpoint Global Outsourcing Inc.
work.summary: Cross-platform mobile internship — contributed to Wookie, Stratpoint's internal workspace-booking app, while completing intensive bootcamps on Scrum, Git, DevOps, QA, and Flutter.
highlights: Wookie (internal workspace-booking app for reserving workstations and scheduling events): contributed feature work that streamlined the booking workflow and improved user experience.
Wookie: collaborated cross-functionally to ensure seamless integration with other Stratpoint internal systems.
Wookie: built robust, maintainable code applying Flutter, Dart, Domain-Driven Design architecture, database management, and API integration.
Engaged in code reviews and testing within an Agile/Scrum workflow on YouTrack — upheld code-quality standards on a team-shipped product.
Completed intensive bootcamps on Scrum, Git, DevOps, QA, and Flutter (basic-to-advanced) during the internship period.

work.name: Coromar Group of Companies
work.summary: Remote frontend contributor on a reservation product — built the front-office web app, a React Native mobile prototype, and site design/prototypes.
highlights: Worked remotely with developers and project managers to deliver modules for a Reservation Website.
Built the front-office reservation application using Next.js and TypeScript.
Prototyped a React Native mobile application for a Coromar Tech client.
Designed and prototyped the company-facing website.

projects.name: Zoomy For Pets — Cedar Tree Brands Inc. e-commerce venture
projects.description: Active venture project where Dave wears both engineer and architect hat to drive the e-commerce application for Cedar Tree Brands Inc.'s Zoomy For Pets storefront. Tech stack centers on Shopify with React + Remix, plus custom Shopify app plugins for analytics. Delivery uses AI-assisted development with Claude Code throughout.
highlights: Engineer + architect on the storefront: own both day-to-day implementation and the architecture decisions for the e-commerce application.
Built on Shopify with a React + Remix front end; integrated custom Shopify app plugins for analytics.
AI-assisted development end-to-end with Claude Code as the primary development tool.

projects.name: Stratpoint AI Enablement Program — First-Cohort Trainer
projects.description: First-cohort internal trainer for AI usage across Stratpoint. Facilitated a 30-person cohort across a 3-week program combining face-to-face sessions and async work. Authored and curated a library of prompt compilations, custom Claude skills, and agents that target repetitive employee tasks so individual contributors can adopt AI without bespoke prompt engineering.
highlights: Selected as one of the first internal trainers responsible for upskilling Stratpoint employees on AI usage.
Facilitated and guided a 30-person cohort across a 3-week program: 3 face-to-face sessions plus 4 async-collaboration days per week.
Built and curated a prompt-compilation, skills, and agent library targeted at repetitive employee tasks.
Designed offerings so non-engineers can adopt AI without bespoke prompt engineering.

projects.name: F1 — Internal Resource Management System (Stratpoint)
projects.description: Internal Stratpoint application for requesting resources. Partial engagement: tasked with revamping the solution and architecture, replacing a React + Parse DB stack with a full-stack Next.js MonoRepo on AWS — PostgreSQL via RDS, AWS SNS for notifications, AWS Cognito for auth, and a GitHub-driven CI/CD pipeline.
highlights: Re-architected F1 from a React + Parse DB stack to a full-stack Next.js MonoRepo on AWS.
Specified PostgreSQL on AWS RDS as the new persistence layer, AWS SNS for notifications, and AWS Cognito for authentication.
Established a GitHub-driven CI/CD pipeline as part of the new architecture.

projects.name: MDM Solution Initiative (Stratpoint internal)
projects.description: Drove Stratpoint's Mobile Device Management (MDM) solution initiative from research through presentation. Evaluated 4 candidate MDM vendors — Sophos, ManageEngine, NinjaOne, and Scalefusion — and delivered the recommendation to internal stakeholders.
highlights: Owned research and analysis across 4 candidate MDM vendors: Sophos, ManageEngine, NinjaOne, and Scalefusion.
Qualified vendors against company requirements and produced trade-off comparisons.
Presented the recommended MDM solution and findings to internal stakeholders.

projects.name: AI Delivery-Process Mapping Initiative (Stratpoint internal)
projects.description: Internal Stratpoint initiative to map the end-to-end delivery process (pre-sales through project closing) and identify where AI integration changes each step.
highlights: Mapped Stratpoint's full delivery process from pre-sales through project closing.
Identified per-step AI integration opportunities and the resulting process changes.
Recommended specific AI tools to apply at each step in the process.

projects.name: Stashville — Gamified Project Tracking Tool for Developers (freelance)
projects.description: Freelance engagement on Stashville, a gamified project-tracking tool for developers. Stack: Pixi.js, Next.js, and Go. Dave owned the daemon and backend, including a custom SDK enabling third-party daemon/extension integration and the daemon↔website connection. AI-assisted development with Claude Code, custom Claude agents Dave built, BMAD workflow, and the Superpowers extension for brainstorming + agent orchestration.
highlights: Owned the daemon and backend for a gamified developer project-tracker built on Pixi.js + Next.js (frontend) and Go (backend).
Designed and shipped a custom SDK so external developers can integrate their own daemons or extensions with Stashville.
Implemented the daemon↔website connection layer.
Delivered with Claude Code + AI-assisted development, leaning on custom Claude agents Dave built himself, the BMAD workflow, and the Superpowers extension for brainstorming and agent orchestration.

projects.name: Hockey Time — Tournament Management Application (freelance)
projects.description: Freelance AI-assisted developer on Hockey Time, a hockey tournament management application. Stack: Next.js, Neon (Postgres), Inngest (durable workflows), and Stripe. Dave built most of the Stripe integration, the CRM workflows, and the hockey scheduling workflows.
highlights: Built the Stripe integration powering payments and billing flows for the tournament platform.
Implemented CRM workflows for managing the platform's customer relationships.
Implemented the hockey-scheduling workflows tying tournaments to matches and timeslots.
Delivered as an AI-assisted developer leveraging modern Next.js + Neon + Inngest patterns.

projects.name: Go1099Pro — Locums Nurse Application (freelance)
projects.description: Freelance React Native developer on Go1099Pro, an application for locums (contract / 1099) nurses covering shifts tracking, shift management, profile management, and expense tracking.
highlights: Built core React Native experiences covering shifts tracking, shift management, profile management, and expense tracking for locums nurses.
Domain: healthcare 1099/contract-worker workflows.

projects.name: Personal Portfolio (v2)
projects.description: Personal portfolio website at portfolio-poc-web.vercel.app. Vite + React 18 SPA with route-based code splitting (per-section JS chunks for About, Experience, Skills, Projects, Testimonials, Contact). Projects section is Strapi-backed (CMS-driven) with a sample-data fallback, devicon icons for the skills grid, and Framer Motion / Embla Carousel for motion + interactions.
highlights: Architected as a Vite + React SPA with per-route code-split chunks for fast first paint and lazy section loading.
Projects section is CMS-driven via Strapi with a graceful sample-data fallback when the CMS is unavailable.
Skills grid uses devicon iconography over a categorized data model (Frontend / Backend / Architecture & DevOps).
Motion + carousel interactions via Framer Motion and Embla.

projects.name: jobhunter (this project)
projects.description: Solo-developer pipeline that ingests pasted job descriptions, tailors a CV + cover letter via a single LLM call, and runs three drift checks (fabrication, content-loss, keyword-stuffing) before notifying the author.
highlights: Walking-skeleton-first delivery: every epic gates on a runnable end-to-end pipeline rather than a tower of unintegrated modules.
Filesystem-only persistence in v1 — no DB — with per-application output slugs under ./out/<slug>/.
Drift checks treat the canonical CV as the source of truth; failures hold the package silently rather than spamming notifications.

projects.name: Wookie — Workspace Booking App (Stratpoint internal)
projects.description: Internal tool for reserving workstations and scheduling events, with real-time availability and integration into Stratpoint's internal systems. Cross-platform Flutter app built during the Stratpoint internship.
highlights: Implemented feature work that streamlined the booking workflow and improved overall user experience.
Built on a Domain-Driven Design architecture for maintainability, with Supabase / Firebase backing and YouTrack-driven Agile/Scrum delivery.
Engaged in code reviews and testing to uphold quality standards on a team-shipped internal product.

projects.name: ChatterBox — Real-time Messaging App
projects.description: Real-time messaging app built during Stratpoint training, applying BLoC state management, DDD architecture, and Firebase-backed auth and messaging.
highlights: Implemented real-time message delivery, user authentication, and presence indicators.
Applied DDD principles for scalable, maintainable architecture.
Used BLoC for state management and GetIt for dependency injection.

projects.name: Moviology — Movie Discovery App
projects.description: Flutter movie-discovery app integrating a third-party REST API to fetch titles, ratings, posters, and metadata, with Riverpod-managed state and a DDD-organized codebase.
highlights: Integrated a third-party REST API (RapidAPI) to fetch and display movie data with search support.
Used Riverpod for efficient state management and a DDD-aligned codebase for maintainability.

projects.name: Remote-Controlled Robotic Toy Car (BSIT Capstone)
projects.description: Capstone project: an Arduino-driven robotic toy car integrated with a precision robotic arm for detecting and collecting lightweight materials from the ground.
highlights: Served as Lead Programmer and Project Manager — owned project timelines and QA compliance through to defense.
Implemented object detection, material-pickup mechanisms, automation controls, and the operator-facing Android control app.
Demonstrated end-to-end problem-solving across embedded firmware (Arduino/C++) and mobile UI (Android).

projects.name: PNP ITMS Hackathon 2022 — Champion
projects.description: Hackathon-winning web+mobile solution built for the PNP Information Technology Management Service. Led ideation, development, and pitch to PNP panelists.
highlights: Spearheaded ideation and proposed the winning strategic solution for the PNP ITMS Hackathon problem statement.
Led development of the web + mobile prototype using ReactJS, Android, and Firebase.
Presented and pitched the solution to PNP panelists, communicating both technical depth and business value.

projects.name: AIA Hackathon 2022 — Finalist
projects.description: Finalist team for the BPI AIA hackathon at BGC Taguig — built a web-based prototype addressing the competition's theme problem.
highlights: Brainstormed and converged on a solution for the BPI AIA hackathon theme.
Developed a web-based competition prototype in ReactJS + Firebase.
Presented and defended the solution to the panelist Q&A.
